edges.description: GATA6 haploinsufficiency disrupts pancreas development, producing absent or hypoplastic endocrine and exocrine tissue.
edges.target: Endocrine and Exocrine Pancreatic Tissue Deficiency

edges.description: PTF1A disruption impairs pancreatic organogenesis, leading to absent pancreatic tissue and combined endocrine-exocrine deficiency.
edges.target: Endocrine and Exocrine Pancreatic Tissue Deficiency

edges.description: Reduced PDX1 protein stability impairs pancreas formation and downstream endocrine and exocrine tissue development.
edges.target: Endocrine and Exocrine Pancreatic Tissue Deficiency

edges.source: PTF1A pathogenic coding or enhancer variants

nodes.description: Absent or markedly hypoplastic pancreas produces severe neonatal insulin deficiency and exocrine pancreatic insufficiency. These downstream consequences drive neonatal diabetes, malabsorption, and the need for insulin and enzyme replacement therapy.
nodes.id: Endocrine and Exocrine Pancreatic Tissue Deficiency

evidence.reference_title: GATA6 mutations cause a broad phenotypic spectrum of diabetes from pancreatic agenesis to adult-onset diabetes without exocrine insufficiency.
evidence.snippet: Pancreatic imaging had been undertaken in 18 of 21 pancreatic agenesis case subjects and showed complete absence (n = 8) or marked hypoplasia (n = 10) of the pancreas.

evidence.reference_title: GATA6 mutations cause a broad phenotypic spectrum of diabetes from pancreatic agenesis to adult-onset diabetes without exocrine insufficiency.
evidence.snippet: Measurement of fecal elastase or fecal fat in 15 case subjects showed severe exocrine pancreatic insufficiency.

nodes.description: Exocrine enzyme deficiency causes maldigestion and requires pancreatic enzyme replacement; steatorrhea can occur when fat absorption is impaired.

evidence.reference_title: GATA6 mutations cause a broad phenotypic spectrum of diabetes from pancreatic agenesis to adult-onset diabetes without exocrine insufficiency.
evidence.snippet: Measurement of fecal elastase or fecal fat in 15 case subjects showed severe exocrine pancreatic insufficiency.

nodes.description: Heterozygous GATA6 loss-of-function variants reduce pancreatic developmental transcription-factor dosage and can cause pancreatic agenesis.

evidence.reference_title: GATA6 haploinsufficiency causes pancreatic agenesis in humans.
evidence.snippet: Therefore, we found GATA6 mutations to be present in the majority (15/27 (56%)) of subjects with pancreatic agenesis.

evidence.reference_title: GATA6 haploinsufficiency causes pancreatic agenesis in humans.
evidence.snippet: The functional studies of missense DNA-binding-domain mutations and the identification of frameshift and splicing mutations suggest that heterozygous GATA6 mutations result in loss of function and cause pancreatic agenesis through haploinsufficiency.

evidence.reference_title: GATA6 haploinsufficiency causes pancreatic agenesis in humans.
evidence.snippet: These findings define the most common cause of human pancreatic agenesis and establish a key role for the transcription factor GATA6 in human pancreatic development.

evidence.reference_title: GATA6 mutations cause a broad phenotypic spectrum of diabetes from pancreatic agenesis to adult-onset diabetes without exocrine insufficiency.
evidence.snippet: In total, we have now identified GATA6 mutations in 21 of 39 (54%) patients with pancreatic agenesis (6 case subjects in this study and 15 reported by Lango Allen et al. [1]).

nodes.description: Insulin replacement treats neonatal diabetes caused by absent or severely reduced endocrine pancreatic tissue.

evidence.reference_title: A Rare PTF1A Enhancer Mutation Causing Neonatal Diabetes Mellitus with Pancreatic Agenesis: Case Report and Considerations for Genetic Evaluation.
evidence.snippet: At two years of age, with pancreatic enzyme replacement and insulin therapy, the patient exhibits normal neurological development, and his physical growth is at the 38th percentile.

nodes.description: Insulin-requiring diabetes usually presents in the first days of life when endocrine pancreatic tissue is absent or severely reduced.

evidence.reference_title: GATA6 mutations cause a broad phenotypic spectrum of diabetes from pancreatic agenesis to adult-onset diabetes without exocrine insufficiency.
evidence.snippet: The median age at diagnosis of diabetes for the 24 probands with GATA6 mutations was 2 days

nodes.description: Biallelic IPF1/PDX1 homeodomain variants can reduce protein stability and transcriptional activity, impairing the pancreatic transcriptional program.

evidence.reference_title: Agenesis of human pancreas due to decreased half-life of insulin promoter factor 1.
evidence.snippet: Here, we describe two novel mutations in the IPF1 gene leading to pancreas agenesis.

evidence.reference_title: Agenesis of human pancreas due to decreased half-life of insulin promoter factor 1.
evidence.snippet: Mutations localized in helices 1 and 2, respectively, of the homeodomain, decreased the protein half-life significantly, leading to intracellular IPF1 levels of 36% and 27% of wild-type levels.

evidence.reference_title: Agenesis of human pancreas due to decreased half-life of insulin promoter factor 1.
evidence.snippet: Here, we describe two novel mutations in the IPF1 gene leading to pancreas agenesis.

nodes.description: Biallelic PTF1A coding, splice-site, or enhancer variants reduce PTF1A activity or expression below the developmental threshold needed for pancreas organogenesis.

evidence.reference_title: GATA6 haploinsufficiency causes pancreatic agenesis in humans.
evidence.snippet: We found one affected subject to have a homozygous PTF1A splice site mutation, but we identified no mutations in PDX1 in this cohort.

evidence.reference_title: A Rare PTF1A Enhancer Mutation Causing Neonatal Diabetes Mellitus with Pancreatic Agenesis: Case Report and Considerations for Genetic Evaluation.
evidence.snippet: Molecular analysis revealed homozygosity for the g.23508437A > G variant within the enhancer region of the PTF1A gene.

nodes.id: PTF1A pathogenic coding or enhancer variants

evidence.reference_title: A Rare PTF1A Enhancer Mutation Causing Neonatal Diabetes Mellitus with Pancreatic Agenesis: Case Report and Considerations for Genetic Evaluation.
evidence.snippet: One of the rarest causes of NDM is pancreatic agenesis, which results from mutations affecting the pancreas transcription factor 1A (PTF1A) gene and its enhancer.

evidence.reference_title: A Rare PTF1A Enhancer Mutation Causing Neonatal Diabetes Mellitus with Pancreatic Agenesis: Case Report and Considerations for Genetic Evaluation.
evidence.snippet: Molecular analysis revealed homozygosity for the g.23508437A > G variant within the enhancer region of the PTF1A gene.

nodes.description: Pancreatic enzyme replacement addresses exocrine pancreatic insufficiency from absent exocrine tissue and supports growth and nutrition.

evidence.reference_title: A Rare PTF1A Enhancer Mutation Causing Neonatal Diabetes Mellitus with Pancreatic Agenesis: Case Report and Considerations for Genetic Evaluation.
evidence.snippet: At two years of age, with pancreatic enzyme replacement and insulin therapy, the patient exhibits normal neurological development, and his physical growth is at the 38th percentile.

nodes.description: Fat malabsorption can accompany exocrine pancreatic insufficiency.

evidence.reference_title: GATA6 mutations cause a broad phenotypic spectrum of diabetes from pancreatic agenesis to adult-onset diabetes without exocrine insufficiency.
evidence.snippet: Measurement of fecal elastase or fecal fat in 15 case subjects showed severe exocrine pancreatic insufficiency.
